name: Rock and Roll
description: Rock 'n' roll icons and bands from many eras

questions.q: Steven Tyler is the flamboyant lead singer of which Boston rock band?
Dave Grohl, former drummer of Nirvana, is the frontman of which rock band?

source.notes: Founded Foo Fighters in 1994 after Kurt Cobain's death.

questions.q: Which troubled singer-songwriter fronted the grunge band Nirvana?

source.notes: Died by suicide in April 1994 at age 27.

questions.q: Robert Plant was the distinctive lead vocalist of which legendary British rock band?
Stevie Nicks became famous as a singer-songwriter in which hugely successful soft-rock band?

source.notes: Joined Fleetwood Mac with Lindsey Buckingham in 1974.

questions.q: Before Brian Johnson took over, who was AC/DC's lead singer who died in 1980?

source.notes: Scott died in London in February 1980; 'Back in Black' was recorded with Brian Johnson shortly after.

questions.q: The haunting post-punk anthem 'Love Will Tear Us Apart' was released in 1980 by which band?

source.notes: Released shortly after singer Ian Curtis's death.

questions.q: The Britpop rivals Oasis hail from which English city?

source.notes: Formed in Manchester in 1991 by brothers Noel and Liam Gallagher.

questions.q: The Who's 1969 rock opera double album is titled what?

source.notes: Tells the story of 'that deaf, dumb, and blind kid'.

questions.q: The evocative 1967 hit 'A Whiter Shade of Pale' was released by which British band?

source.notes: Keith Reid's lyrics and Matthew Fisher's Bach-inspired organ motif.

questions.q: Billy Corgan fronted which 1990s alternative rock band named after a seasonal vegetable?

source.notes: Formed in Chicago 1988; hits include 'Tonight, Tonight' and '1979'.

questions.q: The 1998 ballad 'I Don't Want to Miss a Thing', from the movie 'Armageddon', was a #1 hit for which band?

source.notes: Written by Diane Warren; Aerosmith's first and only US #1.

questions.q: Which British band, featuring Mick Jagger and Keith Richards, recorded 'Paint It Black' and '(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction'?
Freddie Mercury was the legendary lead singer of which British rock band?
Which band's 1976 album 'Hotel California' features a song of the same name about a luxurious but sinister hotel?
Bono is the lead vocalist of which Irish rock band formed in Dublin in 1976?
Which guitarist, widely regarded as one of the greatest of all time, performed 'Purple Haze' and a famous rendition of 'The Star-Spangled Banner' at Woodstock?
Bruce Springsteen is famously nicknamed what?
Which band released the 1987 album 'Appetite for Destruction', featuring the hit 'Sweet Child o' Mine'?
Jim Morrison was the charismatic frontman of which 1960s American rock band?
Which Liverpool band, often called the most influential of all time, included John Lennon and Paul McCartney?
Which band's frontman Chris Martin sang the hit 'Yellow' from their 2000 debut album 'Parachutes'?
Angus Young, known for his schoolboy uniform stage outfit, is the lead guitarist of which Australian band?
Which 1970s band was fronted by Freddie Mercury's contemporary Robert Plant and guitarist Jimmy Page, and recorded 'Stairway to Heaven'?
Which band released the influential 1973 concept album 'The Dark Side of the Moon'?
Ozzy Osbourne was the original lead singer of which pioneering heavy metal band from Birmingham, England?
Which 1991 album by Nirvana, featuring 'Smells Like Teen Spirit', helped bring grunge to the mainstream?
Patti Smith's 1975 debut album, considered a landmark of punk and proto-punk, was titled what?
Which band, fronted by Eddie Vedder, released the 1991 album 'Ten' that became a cornerstone of the Seattle grunge scene?
The guitar riff in Deep Purple's 'Smoke on the Water' was inspired by a real fire at a casino in which Swiss town?
Which American singer-songwriter recorded the 1965 protest-folk-rock track 'Like a Rolling Stone'?
Lou Reed was the principal songwriter and frontman of which influential 1960s New York band associated with Andy Warhol?
Which guitarist co-founded both the Yardbirds' successor act and Led Zeppelin, and played the famous double-neck guitar live?
Captain Beefheart's avant-garde 1969 double album, now considered an experimental rock classic, was titled what?
Who was the original lead singer of Genesis before he left in 1975 to pursue a solo career?
Which band recorded the influential 1969 album 'In the Court of the Crimson King', often cited as a founding work of progressive rock?
The Allman Brothers Band's slide guitarist Duane Allman died in 1971 in an accident involving what vehicle?
Which German band, pioneers of electronic and 'krautrock' sound, released the influential 1974 album 'Autobahn'?
Big Star, a 1970s power-pop band hugely influential on later indie rock, was co-founded by which singer-songwriter who had earlier sung 'The Letter' with the Box Tops?
Which Canadian power trio released the 1976 concept album '2112', built around a lengthy title suite?
Syd Barrett, the original frontman who named Pink Floyd, was largely replaced in the band by which guitarist who joined in early 1968?
The MC5, a fiery proto-punk band, hailed from which American city?
Which band released the 1971 album 'Who's Next', featuring the synthesizer-driven opener 'Baba O'Riley'?
Television's landmark 1977 debut album, a cornerstone of New York's CBGB scene, was titled what?
Which English rock band, fronted by Roger Daltrey and Pete Townshend, was originally named The High Numbers before adopting their famous mod-era moniker?
